title: Zichya dilatata
taxonomy: Zichya dilatata is classified as a species of annual plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Zichya. The species is native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. Zichya dilatata is further classified as Zichya dilatata (Boiss.) T. Durieu, indicating its botanical name. This classification helps identify the plant within the broader kingdom of Plantae.

intro: Zichya dilatata, also known as Zichya or Egyptian millet, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and lowering blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Zichya dilatata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Zichya is often prepared as a tea, powder, or extract, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
